text: Human memory is not a recording device — it's reconstructive and fallible. Types: sensory (milliseconds), short-term/working (seconds, 7±2 items — Miller, 1956), long-term (unlimited capacity). Long-term: explicit (declarative: episodic + semantic) and implicit (procedural skills, conditioning). Consolidation: moving memories from hippocampus to co
Working memory: 4±1 chunks (Cowan, 2001 — updated from Miller).
